content: {"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/22790477/", "source_title": "Effect of Thermal Aging on the Tensile Bond Strength at Reduced Areas of Seven Current Adhesives", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.5, "overall_relevance": 0.2, "year": 2012, "authors": [], "venue": "Odontology", "doi": "10.1007/s10266-012-0073-2", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "Thermal aging by 5,000 thermocycles induced a significant decrease in micro-tensile bond strength values for all seven adhesive systems tested, with one-step self-etch adhesives showing the lowest results.", "direct_quote": "Thermal aging induced a significant decrease in MTBS values with all adhesives tested. The resistance of resin-dentin bonds to thermal-aging degradation was material dependent. One-step self-etch adhesives obtained the lowest MTBS results after both aging treatments, and their adhesive capacity was significantly reduced after thermocycling", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.25, "confidence": 0.87, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["micro-tensile bond strength", "thermocycling", "5000 cycles", "self-etch adhesives"]}, {"statement": "After 24-hour water storage at 37 C, total-etch adhesives XP Bond and Adper Scotchbond 1 XT showed highest MTBS values, while one-step self-etch adhesives Bond Force, Xeno V, and G-Bond were lowest.", "direct_quote": "After 24 h water storage, MTBS values were highest with XP Bond, Adper Scotchbond 1 XT, Filtek Silorane Adhesive System and Adper Scotchbond SE and lowest with the one-step self-etch adhesives Bond Force, Xeno V and G-Bond. After thermocycling, MTBS values were highest with XP Bond", "fact_category": "measurement", "relevance_score": 0.18, "confidence": 0.88, "perspective": "Methodological", "entities": ["XP Bond", "Adper Scotchbond", "MTBS", "water storage", "37 C"]}], "evidence_summary": "Dental adhesion study on thermal aging effects on seven dental adhesive systems.
